title: When Thinking About Other Groups: - Pause Assumptions: Ask Yourself,
subtitle: See the Individual, Not the Group
description:
When thinking about other groups:
- Pause assumptions: Ask yourself, "Am I generalizing based on the group?"
- Get personal: Interact with individuals from the group to learn about their unique experiences.
- Stay curious: Challenge stereotypes by seeking real, diverse examples.

Example: If you think "All people from X group act this way," question that thought and find real-world exceptions.

howTo.title: Pause and Reflect
howTo.summary: Before your next interaction, take a moment to pause and ask yourself if you are generalizing based on group characteristics. This brief reflection helps you become aware of potential biases.

howTo.title: Ask Clarifying Questions
howTo.summary: If you recognize a generalization, follow up with a specific clarifying question to elicit individual experiences. This encourages deeper understanding and reduces reliance on stereotypes.

howTo.title: Observe Individual Attributes
howTo.summary: During social interactions, focus on identifying unique attributes of individuals rather than relying on group labels. This practice fosters curiosity and enhances interpersonal connections.

howTo.title: Log Your Insights
howTo.summary: After interactions, take a moment to record what you learned about individuals and any changes in your assumptions. This helps reinforce the habit of questioning stereotypes.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results and reflections in the Brali LifeOS app to track your progress and insights. Use this link to access the app: https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/challenge-stereotypes-coach/.
